集训3 · 假期复习任务卡 · Day 23 of 24

The Wilderness Weekend

📖 Reading

319 words · 28 target words · listen while you read

Tier 1（11词） Tier 2（7词） Tier 3（10词）

Owen had lived in the city his whole life, so when his older cousin invited him to attend his first outdoor adventure weekend, he felt both excited and nervous. He packed his bag with everything on the list: a flashlight, a rope, and a small pouch of tools.

At the campsite, the leader, Mr. Diaz, welcomed every new member with a warm handshake. He was patient and trustworthy, the kind of adult who made hard things feel manageable. "Stay close to your partner," he said, "and always be polite to your team."

The first activity seemed easy: mix dried fruit and nuts to make trail snacks for the hike. Owen enjoyed it, humming quietly with pleasure as he worked.

Soon the group set off through the forest toward the base of the mountain. Owen and his partner had to search for five colored markers hidden along the trail. Sunlight would shine through the leaves in bright patches, making the hunt feel like a game.

Near a river crossing, the path became narrow and a little dangerous, with slick rocks near the water. Mr. Diaz asked everyone to hold the rope with both hands while crossing. Owen felt a flicker of fear — what if he slipped? Mr. Diaz reminded them that if they ever felt afraid, the best skill was staying calm instead of rushing. Owen took a slow breath and did not freeze; he simply stepped carefully, one rock at a time, until he reached the other side.

By the end of the hike, Owen could not find the right word to describe how proud he felt. The stress of the morning had faded completely, and everything felt fine. Mr. Diaz told him he had done something remarkable and shown true care for his teammates that day.

That night, Owen realized he was growing more confident with every new challenge, and he could not wait to come back.

🔍 Reading Comprehension

5 questions · tap to check your answer

1In "He was patient and trustworthy, the kind of adult who made hard things feel manageable", what does patient mean?
A — patient means able to wait calmly, which matches Mr. Diaz making hard things feel manageable.
2In "He was patient and trustworthy, the kind of adult who made hard things feel manageable", what does trustworthy mean?
A — trustworthy means able to be trusted, which is why the campers felt safe following Mr. Diaz.
3In "Owen took a slow breath and did not freeze; he simply stepped carefully, one rock at a time", what does freeze mean?
B — freeze here means to stop moving suddenly from fear, which Owen avoided by staying calm and moving carefully.
4What did Mr. Diaz have everyone do before crossing the river?
B — the story says Mr. Diaz asked everyone to hold the rope with both hands while crossing the river.
5How did Owen feel by the end of the story, after crossing the river safely?
C — the stress faded, everything felt fine, and Owen realized he was growing more confident with every challenge.
🔁
